title: undo-journal rollback implementation (with #756)
manager: The actual rollback mechanism: an undo journal that records each assignment so a failed macrostep can be reversed exactly, restoring the machine to its last stable state. This is what makes FSL's transactional guarantee real — a macrostep either commits wholly or leaves no trace — and it rides the same columnar-val journal as the performance keystone, so correctness and speed share one implementation instead of two that drift apart.
cs: undo-log journal; reverse-replay on violation to the last stable state; shares the columnar-val journal (Tjssm-756); the mechanism behind the rollback event (W6.68).
